clients.name: Claude Code
clients.description: Anthropic's agentic command-line coding tool, deeply integrated with the terminal and your codebase.

mcp.summary: Add MCP servers via claude mcp add or .mcp.json. Three scopes (local/project/user), stdio/HTTP/SSE/WebSocket transports, OAuth, and tool search.

files.note: Local scope nests under projects.<path>.mcpServers; user scope is top-level.

fields.name: type
fields.description: Transport type. 'streamable-http' is accepted as an alias for 'http'.

fields.name: command
fields.description: Executable for a stdio server.

fields.name: args
fields.description: Arguments for the stdio command.

fields.name: env
fields.description: Environment variables. Supports ${VAR} and ${VAR:-default} expansion.

fields.name: url
fields.description: Endpoint for HTTP/SSE/WebSocket servers.

fields.name: headers
fields.description: Static HTTP headers (e.g. Authorization). Supports env expansion.

fields.name: headersHelper
fields.description: Command that prints a JSON object of headers at connect time (dynamic auth).

fields.name: oauth
fields.description: OAuth configuration for remote servers.

fields.name: timeout
fields.description: Per-server tool execution timeout in milliseconds.

fields.name: alwaysLoad
fields.description: Exempt this server's tools from deferral so they load at session start.

capabilities.name: Roots
capabilities.note: Claude Code answers roots/list with the project directory.

capabilities.name: Elicitation
capabilities.note: Form and URL elicitation dialogs appear automatically.

notes: Project scope uses .mcp.json (checked into version control, requires approval).
Local and user scopes are stored in ~/.claude.json; type 'streamable-http' is an alias for 'http'.
SSE transport is deprecated in favor of HTTP.

skills.summary: SKILL.md skills with rich frontmatter, invoked with /name or loaded automatically by Claude. Custom commands are merged into skills.

files.note: Personal skills, available across all projects.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Display name. Defaults to the directory name.

fields.name: description
fields.description: What the skill does and when to use it. Recommended.

fields.name: when_to_use
fields.description: Extra trigger context appended to description in the skill listing.

fields.name: argument-hint
fields.description: Autocomplete hint for expected arguments.

fields.name: arguments
fields.description: Named positional arguments for $name substitution.

fields.name: disable-model-invocation
fields.description: When true, only the user can invoke (not Claude). Default false.

fields.name: user-invocable
fields.description: When false, hide from the / menu (Claude-only). Default true.

fields.name: allowed-tools
fields.description: Tools usable without approval while the skill is active.

fields.name: disallowed-tools
fields.description: Tools removed from the pool while the skill is active.

fields.name: model
fields.description: Model to use while the skill is active, or 'inherit'.

fields.name: effort
fields.description: Effort level override while the skill is active.

fields.name: context
fields.description: Run the skill in a forked subagent context.

fields.name: agent
fields.description: Subagent type to use when context: fork is set.

fields.name: hooks
fields.description: Hooks scoped to the skill's lifecycle.

fields.name: paths
fields.description: Glob patterns that limit when the skill auto-activates.

fields.name: shell
fields.description: Shell for inline !`command` blocks. Default bash.

examples.title: Task skill (manual invocation)
examples.code:
---
name: deploy
description: Deploy the application to production
disable-model-invocation: true
allowed-tools: Bash(git add *) Bash(git commit *)
---

Deploy $ARGUMENTS to production:
1. Run the test suite
2. Build and push

notes: Live change detection reloads SKILL.md edits within a session.
Project skills load from .claude/skills/ up the directory tree and from nested dirs on demand.

rules.summary: CLAUDE.md memory files (project/user/managed) plus modular .claude/rules/*.md with optional path scoping. Auto memory accumulates learnings.

files.note: Also reads ./.claude/CLAUDE.md. CLAUDE.local.md for gitignored personal notes.
Modular topic rules; recursive. Path-scope with the paths frontmatter field.
Personal instructions for all projects. ~/.claude/rules/*.md also supported.
Managed policy (macOS). Linux/WSL: /etc/claude-code/CLAUDE.md; Windows: C:\Program Files\ClaudeCode\CLAUDE.md.

fields.name: paths
fields.description: Glob patterns; a rule file applies only when matching files are in context.

examples.title: Path-scoped rule
examples.code:
---
paths:
  - "src/api/**/*.ts"
---

# API Development Rules
- All API endpoints must include input validation
- Use the standard error response format

notes: Claude Code reads CLAUDE.md, not AGENTS.md. Import an existing AGENTS.md with @AGENTS.md inside CLAUDE.md.
Auto memory is stored per repo in ~/.claude/projects/<project>/memory/MEMORY.md.
Use @path/to/file imports (max depth 4) to compose instructions.

hooks.summary: Shell commands that fire on lifecycle events, configured in settings.json under the hooks key (or scoped to a skill/subagent).

fields.name: matcher
fields.description: Pattern matched against tool name (for PreToolUse/PostToolUse) to scope the hook.

fields.name: hooks[].type
fields.description: Hook handler type.

fields.name: hooks[].command
fields.description: Shell command to run. Receives event JSON on stdin.

fields.name: hooks[].timeout
fields.description: Optional timeout in seconds for the hook command.

commands.summary: Custom slash commands as markdown files. Merged into Skills — a command and a skill of the same name behave identically; skills add supporting files and auto-invocation.

fields.name: description
fields.description: What the command does.

fields.name: argument-hint
fields.description: Autocomplete hint for expected arguments.

fields.name: allowed-tools
fields.description: Tools the command may use without approval.

fields.name: model
fields.description: Model override while the command runs.

notes: .claude/commands/<name>.md creates /<name> and supports the same frontmatter as skills.
Skills are recommended for new workflows since they support supporting files and model invocation.

settings.summary: Hierarchical settings.json controls permissions, environment, hooks, model, and skill behavior across user, project, local, and managed scopes.

files.note: Shared project settings (checked in).
Personal project settings (gitignored).
User settings for all projects.
Managed policy settings deployed by IT/DevOps.

fields.name: permissions
fields.type: { allow?: string[], deny?: string[], ask?: string[], additionalDirectories?: string[] }
fields.description: Allow/deny/ask rules governing tool and command use.

fields.name: env
fields.description: Environment variables applied to every session.

fields.name: hooks
fields.description: Lifecycle hook configuration (see Hooks).

fields.name: model
fields.description: Default model for sessions.

fields.name: disableBundledSkills
fields.description: Disable the built-in bundled skills (/code-review, /debug, etc.).

fields.name: skillOverrides
fields.description: Override skill visibility without editing SKILL.md frontmatter.

fields.name: autoMemoryEnabled
fields.description: Toggle auto memory. Default true.

fields.name: claudeMd
fields.description: Managed CLAUDE.md content (managed/policy scope only).

mcp.summary: Local (stdio) MCP servers in JSON under the mcpServers key.

files.note: macOS path; see Claude Desktop docs for other operating systems.

fields.name: command
fields.description: Command to start a local (stdio) server.

fields.name: args
fields.description: Arguments passed to the command.

fields.name: env
fields.description: Environment variables for the server.

notes: Only local (stdio) servers are configured via the file. Add remote servers through Settings → Connectors in the app.

mcp.summary: Configure MCP servers in JSON under the mcpServers key.

files.note: Stored under the VS Code globalStorage directory (e.g. ~/Library/Application Support/Code/User/globalStorage on macOS).

fields.name: command
fields.description: Command to start a local (stdio) server.

fields.name: args
fields.description: Arguments passed to the command.

fields.name: env
fields.description: Environment variables for the server.

fields.name: url
fields.description: Endpoint URL for a remote server.

fields.name: type
fields.description: Remote transport type.

fields.name: disabled
fields.description: Whether the server is disabled.

fields.name: headers
fields.description: HTTP headers for a remote server.

notes: Remote entries set type to 'sse' or 'streamableHttp' and include disabled.

fields.name: description

notes: Skill discovery paths from the open skills CLI (vercel-labs/skills).

files.note: $CLINE_DIR defaults to ~/.cline.

fields.name: command
fields.description: Command to start a local (stdio) server.

fields.name: args
fields.description: Arguments passed to the command.

fields.name: env
fields.description: Environment variables for the server.

fields.name: url
fields.description: Endpoint URL for a remote server.

fields.name: type
fields.description: Remote transport type.

fields.name: disabled
fields.description: Whether the server is disabled.

fields.name: headers
fields.description: HTTP headers for a remote server.

clients.name: Codex
clients.description: OpenAI's coding agent for the terminal and IDE, configured through a single config.toml.

mcp.summary: MCP servers are defined as [mcp_servers.<name>] tables in config.toml. Supports stdio and Streamable HTTP, with bearer-token and OAuth auth.

files.note: Trusted projects only.

fields.name: command
fields.description: Launcher command for a stdio server.

fields.name: args
fields.description: Arguments passed to the stdio command.

fields.name: env
fields.description: Environment variables forwarded to the stdio server.

fields.name: env_vars
fields.description: Additional env vars to allow/forward. source = local | remote.

fields.name: cwd
fields.description: Working directory for the stdio server process.

fields.name: url
fields.description: Endpoint for a Streamable HTTP server.

fields.name: bearer_token_env_var
fields.description: Env var name holding a bearer token sent in Authorization.

fields.name: http_headers
fields.description: Static HTTP headers for each request.

fields.name: env_http_headers
fields.description: HTTP headers populated from environment variables.

fields.name: enabled
fields.description: Disable a server without removing its config.

fields.name: startup_timeout_sec
fields.description: Server startup timeout (default 10s).

fields.name: tool_timeout_sec
fields.description: Per-tool execution timeout (default 60s).

fields.name: enabled_tools
fields.description: Allow list of tool names exposed by the server.

fields.name: disabled_tools
fields.description: Deny list applied after enabled_tools.

fields.name: scopes
fields.description: OAuth scopes to request when authenticating.

capabilities.name: Server instructions
capabilities.note: Codex reads the MCP `instructions` field as server-wide guidance.

notes: The CLI and IDE extension share this configuration. Manage servers with `codex mcp`.
Project-scoped .codex/config.toml is loaded only for trusted projects.

skills.summary: SKILL.md skills following the open Agent Skills standard, discovered from .agents/skills up to the repo root. Invoke with $ or /skills.

files.note: Scanned from CWD up to repo root.
User skills applied across all repositories.
Admin/system skills shared on the machine.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Skill identifier.

fields.name: description
fields.description: When the skill should and should not trigger.

examples.title: Minimal SKILL.md
examples.code:
---
name: skill-name
description: Explain exactly when this skill should and should not trigger.
---

Skill instructions for Codex to follow.

notes: Codex scans .agents/skills in every directory from CWD up to the repository root.
Optional agents/openai.yaml adds UI metadata and policy.allow_implicit_invocation.
Disable a skill via [[skills.config]] (path + enabled) in config.toml.

rules.summary: AGENTS.md instruction files, layered from the Codex home directory down to the working directory, with override files and configurable fallbacks.

files.note: Project root down to CWD, including nested directories. AGENTS.override.md takes precedence.
Global defaults inherited by every repository. ~/.codex/AGENTS.override.md for temporary overrides.

examples.title: Global guidance (~/.codex/AGENTS.md)
examples.code:
## Working agreements

- Always run `npm test` after modifying JavaScript files.
- Prefer `pnpm` when installing dependencies.

notes: Codex reads AGENTS.override.md before AGENTS.md at each level; only one file per directory is used.
Files merge root-first; closer files override earlier guidance.
project_doc_max_bytes (default 32 KiB) caps combined size; project_doc_fallback_filenames adds alternate names.

hooks.summary: Lifecycle hooks loaded from hooks.json or an inline [hooks] table in config.toml. Gated behind the features.hooks flag; command hooks are executed.

files.note: Inline [hooks] table, or a separate hooks.json.
Trusted projects only.

fields.name: hooks.<event>
fields.description: Matcher groups for a hook event.

fields.name: hooks.<event>[].hooks
fields.description: Hook handlers for a matcher group (command hooks supported).

fields.name: hooks.<event>[].hooks[].command
fields.description: Command to run for a command hook.

fields.name: hooks.<event>[].hooks[].commandWindows
fields.description: Windows-only command override (alias command_windows).

notes: Enable with features.hooks = true (deprecated alias: features.codex_hooks).
Command hooks are supported; prompt and agent hook handlers are parsed but skipped.
Admins can enforce managed hooks via requirements.toml and allow_managed_hooks_only.

commands.summary: Custom prompts (~/.codex/prompts/*.md, invoked /prompts:name) were deprecated and removed in codex-cli 0.117.0. Convert custom prompts to Skills.
notes: Codex still ships built-in slash commands (/review, /fork, /plan, /diff, /compact, etc.).
For reusable custom workflows, author Skills instead.

settings.summary: config.toml is the single settings file for models, reasoning, sandboxing, approvals, and tools. requirements.toml enforces admin policy.

files.note: Trusted projects only; provider/auth/telemetry keys are ignored here.
Admin-enforced constraints on security-sensitive settings.

fields.name: model
fields.description: Model slug Codex uses for turns.

fields.name: model_reasoning_effort
fields.description: Reasoning effort for supported models.

fields.name: approval_policy
fields.description: When Codex asks for command approval.

fields.name: sandbox_mode
fields.description: Filesystem/network sandbox for tool calls.

fields.name: default_permissions
fields.description: Named permissions profile (:read-only, :workspace, :danger-full-access, or custom).

fields.name: web_search
fields.description: Web search mode (default cached).

fields.name: file_opener
fields.description: URI scheme used to open citations from Codex output.

notes: Project-scoped .codex/config.toml can't override provider, auth, notification, or telemetry keys.
Install the Even Better TOML extension and add #:schema https://developers.openai.com/codex/config-schema.json for autocompletion.

skills.summary: SKILL.md skills following the open Agent Skills standard.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Skill identifier (required by the Agent Skills standard).

fields.name: description
fields.description: What the skill does and when to use it.

notes: Skill discovery paths from the open skills CLI (vercel-labs/skills).

clients.name: Cursor
clients.description: The AI code editor. Agent, Tab, and Inline Edit backed by frontier models.

mcp.summary: Configure MCP servers via mcp.json. Supports stdio, SSE, and Streamable HTTP, with OAuth and config interpolation.

fields.name: type
fields.description: Server connection type for local command servers.

fields.name: command
fields.description: Command to start a stdio server executable (e.g. npx, node, python, docker).

fields.name: args
fields.description: Arguments passed to the command.

fields.name: env
fields.description: Environment variables for the server. Supports ${env:NAME} interpolation.

fields.name: envFile
fields.description: Path to an environment file to load (stdio servers only).

fields.name: url
fields.description: Endpoint URL for a remote HTTP or SSE server.

fields.name: headers
fields.description: HTTP headers for remote servers (e.g. Authorization).

fields.name: auth
fields.type: { CLIENT_ID: string, CLIENT_SECRET?: string, scopes?: string[] }
fields.description: Static OAuth client credentials for remote servers.

capabilities.name: Apps
capabilities.note: Interactive UI views returned by MCP tools (MCP Apps extension).

skills.summary: SKILL.md skills loaded from .cursor/skills and .agents/skills, invoked with / or @. Also reads Claude and Codex skill dirs.

files.note: Shared agent-skills directory (cross-tool standard).

fields.name: name
fields.description: Skill name. Defaults to the containing folder name.

fields.name: description
fields.description: What the skill does; helps Agent decide when to load it.

fields.name: paths
fields.description: Glob patterns that scope the skill to matching files.

examples.title: Scoped skill
examples.code:
---
name: react-component-patterns
description: Conventions for writing React components.
paths:
  - "**/*.tsx"
---

# React component patterns
...

notes: For compatibility, Cursor also loads skills from .claude/skills, .codex/skills, ~/.claude/skills, and ~/.codex/skills.
Skills in nested project dirs (e.g. apps/web/.cursor/skills/) are scoped to files in that dir.

rules.summary: Project rules as .mdc files with frontmatter, plus plain-markdown AGENTS.md (root and nested).

files.note: Version-controlled, scoped via path patterns. Organize in subfolders.
Plain-markdown instructions in project root and any subdirectory (nested).

fields.name: description
fields.description: When set with alwaysApply false, Agent pulls the rule in when relevant.

fields.name: globs
fields.description: Comma-separated glob patterns; auto-attaches the rule for matching files.

fields.name: alwaysApply
fields.description: When true, the rule is included in every chat session.

examples.title: Always-applied rule (.mdc)
examples.code:
---
alwaysApply: true
---

- All source files must include the company copyright header
- Never modify generated files in dist/ or build/

hooks.summary: hooks.json defines scripts that observe, block, or modify the agent loop. Command and prompt hook types. Claude Code hook compatible.

files.note: macOS MDM path. Linux/WSL: /etc/cursor/hooks.json; Windows: C:\ProgramData\Cursor\hooks.json.

fields.name: version
fields.description: Config schema version (currently 1).

fields.name: command
fields.description: Script path or shell command (for command hooks).

fields.name: type
fields.description: Hook execution type. Defaults to command.

fields.name: timeout
fields.description: Execution timeout in seconds.

fields.name: loop_limit
fields.description: Per-script loop limit for stop/subagentStop hooks. Default 5 for Cursor.

fields.name: failClosed
fields.description: When true, hook failures block the action instead of failing open.

fields.name: matcher
fields.description: Filter criteria (tool name, subagent type, or command pattern) for when the hook runs.

commands.summary: Cursor merged slash commands into Skills. Existing user- and workspace-level commands can be converted with /migrate-to-skills; author new workflows as Skills.
notes: Both user-level and workspace-level commands are convertible to skills (Cursor 2.4+).
Use the Skills surface for new reusable workflows.

settings.summary: Most settings live in the Cursor Settings UI. permissions.json controls Agent Run Mode allowlists and MCP auto-run behavior.

files.note: Allowlist MCP tools and shell commands for Run Mode.

notes: Pre-configure which MCP tools run without approval by adding them to permissions.json.
autoRun instructions in permissions.json steer the Auto-review classifier per server or tool.

mcp.summary: Configure MCP servers in JSON. Global uses mcpServers; project uses the VS Code servers key.

files.note: Or $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/mcp-config.json.
Project config uses the VS Code servers key.

fields.name: type
fields.description: Server transport type.

fields.name: command
fields.description: Command to start a local (stdio) server.

fields.name: args
fields.description: Arguments passed to the command.

fields.name: env
fields.description: Environment variables for the server.

fields.name: url
fields.description: Endpoint URL for a remote server.

fields.name: headers
fields.description: HTTP headers for a remote server.

fields.name: tools
fields.description: Tool allow-list; global entries use ["*"] for all tools.

mcp.summary: Configure MCP servers in YAML under the extensions key.

files.note: Windows: %APPDATA%/Block/goose/config/config.yaml.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Extension name.

fields.name: cmd
fields.description: Command for a local (stdio) extension.

fields.name: args
fields.description: Arguments passed to the command.

fields.name: uri
fields.description: Endpoint URL for a remote extension.

fields.name: type
fields.description: Extension transport type.

fields.name: enabled
fields.description: Whether the extension is enabled.

fields.name: envs
fields.description: Environment variables.

fields.name: timeout
fields.description: Timeout in seconds.

notes: Servers are configured as entries under the extensions key (not mcpServers).

mcp.summary: Configure MCP servers in JSON under the mcp key.

fields.name: type
fields.description: Server type.

fields.name: command
fields.description: For local servers: [command, ...args].

fields.name: environment
fields.description: Environment variables for local servers.

fields.name: url
fields.description: Endpoint URL for a remote server.

fields.name: headers
fields.description: HTTP headers for a remote server.

fields.name: enabled
fields.description: Whether the server is enabled.

notes: Local servers use type 'local' with command as an array; remote servers use type 'remote' with url.

mcp.summary: Configure MCP servers in JSON under the servers key.

files.note: e.g. ~/Library/Application Support/Code/User/mcp.json on macOS.

fields.name: type
fields.description: Remote transport type.

fields.name: command
fields.description: Command to start a local (stdio) server.

fields.name: args
fields.description: Arguments passed to the command.

fields.name: env
fields.description: Environment variables for the server.

fields.name: url
fields.description: Endpoint URL for a remote server.

fields.name: headers
fields.description: HTTP headers for a remote server.

notes: Servers live under the servers key (not mcpServers).

fields.name: url
fields.description: Endpoint URL for a remote server.

fields.name: headers

notes: Servers live under context_servers; entries set source: "custom".
